# Nightly whitebox-e2e run: unattended execution + red-report A2A digest

## Context
Operator gap-hunt (2026-07-04). e2e lives OUTSIDE CI by design (failing-honest discipline; `NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI` owns exclusions) — but nothing RUNS it on a schedule. With the sprint landing dock e2e (#14591), FM module NL-proofs (#14563-class), and tour-replay specs, the suite grows while its execution stays manual: red states will sit undiscovered exactly like the middleware staleness did (3 weeks, nobody looked).

## The Problem
Post-fable, the quality heartbeat must be unattended: run the whitebox-e2e configs nightly against dev, and deliver a RED-ONLY digest into the A2A mailbox (normal-priority, wake-tier compliant per #14576) naming the failing specs + first-error lines — red-as-pointer made push, not pull.

## The Architectural Reality
Sibling precedent: the middleware 8h LaunchAgent (staged plist + hardened runner, neomjs/neo#12964 pattern — lockfile, clean-tree gate, structured logs, ChainAbort/finally hygiene). The A2A send rides the existing MessageService (system-sender class exists: @system identity). CRITICAL inherited rule: custom playwright configs only, never default `npx playwright test`.

## The Fix
One PR: a scheduled runner script (LaunchAgent-staged, the #12964 hardening pattern) executing the named e2e configs → parse results → on-red: one A2A digest (failing spec list + first errors + run log path); on-green: silence (no noise). Config list declared in the script header (additive as suites land).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unattended run executes all declared configs; lockfile + abort hygiene per the #12964 pattern.
- [ ] Red digest arrives via A2A (normal priority, mailbox-drain class — never a wake storm); green runs send nothing.
- [ ] Digest names specs + first-error lines + log path (actionable without archaeology).
- [ ] Staged-not-auto-installed plist with activation docs.
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope
CI integration (the outside-CI discipline stands) · fixing reds (the digest points, owners fix) · unit suites (CI owns those).

## Related
#14591 + #14563-class consumers · middleware scheduler precedent (neomjs/neo#12964, PR middleware-v2#17) · #14576 wake tiers · failing-honest discipline (operator-corrected precedent). Sweeps: epic+search 04:02Z, no equivalent.

- 2026-07-04T12:48:07Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `075df80` - "test(ai): extract nightly-e2e pure digest core + unit-cover it (#14685)

Commit 2: split the pure parse/format logic (collectFailures, isRed, formatDigest) into the service-free
nightlyE2eDigest.mjs so it unit-tests without booting the memory-core services the runner imports. The runner
now delegates to it (single source). 7 specs green: nested-suite failure collection, all-green → [], fail-open
on malformed reports, first-error truncation, isRed (failing spec | infra-red ran:false | clean-green), digest
formatting (names config/spec/error/log-path, skips clean-green), infra-red surfacing.

Co-Authored-By: Ada (Claude Opus 4.8) <ada@neomjs.dev>"
- 2026-07-04T13:10:01Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `0809ce8` - "feat(ai): nightly-e2e staged LaunchAgent plist + activation README (#14685)

Commit 3: the staged-not-auto-installed plist template (com.neomjs.nightly-e2e.plist — __NEO_REPO_ROOT__
placeholder, RunAtLoad false, nightly 03:17, ProcessType Background) + the activation README (render/install via
launchctl bootstrap, kickstart smoke-test, read last-run.json + the @system RED digest, add-a-suite, uninstall).
Why staged: the plist carries a machine-specific repo path + operator-owned schedule — auto-installing from a
checkout would be a surprising side effect. Completes the #14685 implementation ACs (runner + digest tests +
plist + docs); cross-family review + the live red/green dry-run remain.

Co-Authored-By: Ada (Claude Opus 4.8) <ada@neomjs.dev>"
- 2026-07-04T15:41:09Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `0d8e484` - "fix(ai): back the nightly-e2e per-run log with a real file (#14685)

runConfig spawned Playwright with stdio:inherit, so the per-run logPath
referenced in last-run.json and the digest's `Run log:` pointer was never
written — a red/infra-red digest could point at a phantom file. runConfig now
captures the child output (pipe) and echoes it through; a pure buildRunLog()
assembles the per-config body; runNightlyE2e ensures the logs dir and writes the
file. Three specs added: log-body content, the no-output fallback, and a
write+readback phantom-log regression (10/10 green).

Addresses @neo-gpt's review on #14754."
- 2026-07-04T16:05:02Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `27959b8` - "fix(ai): clear stale e2e reporter output so it can't suppress a red digest (#14685)

runConfig read back entry.results without clearing it first, so a prior run's
stale (possibly green) results.json could be scored as THIS run's result — an
infra failure that writes no fresh report would report green, silently
suppressing the nightly red digest (the heartbeat's one job is to not go
silent). runConfig now fs.removeSync(entry.results) before spawning, so a
no-fresh-report run correctly falls to the infra-red path. spawn is now an
injectable seam; two specs prove a stale green cannot suppress a red and that a
fresh report is still read (12/12 green in the lifecycle suite).

Addresses @neo-gpt's re-review on #14754."
- 2026-07-04T16:18:43Z @tobiu referenced in commit `3652ad8` - "feat(ai): nightly whitebox-e2e runner + red-only A2A digest (#14685) (#14754)

* feat(ai): nightly whitebox-e2e runner core — red-only A2A digest (#14685)

Commit 1 of the unattended quality heartbeat: e2e lives outside CI by design (failing-honest discipline), but
nothing ran it on a schedule, so reds sat undiscovered. runNightlyE2e() holds an exclusive stale-steal PID
lockfile, runs the header-declared whitebox-e2e configs (custom -c only, never default npx playwright test),
parses each json reporter output, and on ANY red pushes exactly ONE normal-priority (mailbox-drain, never a
wake storm) A2A digest to AGENT:* via the @system/MailboxService pattern — naming failing specs + first-error
lines + the run-log path. Green runs are silent. A non-zero exit with no report is surfaced as an infra red,
not swallowed as green. finally-hygiene always releases the lock.

Placement: ai/scripts/lifecycle/ (sibling to swarmWakeCooldown.mjs — the lifecycle-script + A2A-send pattern
lifted verbatim). Follows the cross-repo middleware-scheduler LaunchAgent hardening pattern.

Remaining ACs (draft PR): unit coverage for the pure parse/format logic, the staged-not-auto-installed plist +
activation README, and cross-family review.

Co-Authored-By: Ada (Claude Opus 4.8) <ada@neomjs.dev>

* test(ai): extract nightly-e2e pure digest core + unit-cover it (#14685)

Commit 2: split the pure parse/format logic (collectFailures, isRed, formatDigest) into the service-free
nightlyE2eDigest.mjs so it unit-tests without booting the memory-core services the runner imports. The runner
now delegates to it (single source). 7 specs green: nested-suite failure collection, all-green → [], fail-open
on malformed reports, first-error truncation, isRed (failing spec | infra-red ran:false | clean-green), digest
formatting (names config/spec/error/log-path, skips clean-green), infra-red surfacing.

Co-Authored-By: Ada (Claude Opus 4.8) <ada@neomjs.dev>

* feat(ai): nightly-e2e staged LaunchAgent plist + activation README (#14685)

Commit 3: the staged-not-auto-installed plist template (com.neomjs.nightly-e2e.plist — __NEO_REPO_ROOT__
placeholder, RunAtLoad false, nightly 03:17, ProcessType Background) + the activation README (render/install via
launchctl bootstrap, kickstart smoke-test, read last-run.json + the @system RED digest, add-a-suite, uninstall).
Why staged: the plist carries a machine-specific repo path + operator-owned schedule — auto-installing from a
checkout would be a surprising side effect. Completes the #14685 implementation ACs (runner + digest tests +
plist + docs); cross-family review + the live red/green dry-run remain.

Co-Authored-By: Ada (Claude Opus 4.8) <ada@neomjs.dev>

* fix(ai): back the nightly-e2e per-run log with a real file (#14685)

runConfig spawned Playwright with stdio:inherit, so the per-run logPath
referenced in last-run.json and the digest's `Run log:` pointer was never
written — a red/infra-red digest could point at a phantom file. runConfig now
captures the child output (pipe) and echoes it through; a pure buildRunLog()
assembles the per-config body; runNightlyE2e ensures the logs dir and writes the
file. Three specs added: log-body content, the no-output fallback, and a
write+readback phantom-log regression (10/10 green).

Addresses @neo-gpt's review on #14754.

* fix(ai): clear stale e2e reporter output so it can't suppress a red digest (#14685)

runConfig read back entry.results without clearing it first, so a prior run's
stale (possibly green) results.json could be scored as THIS run's result — an
infra failure that writes no fresh report would report green, silently
suppressing the nightly red digest (the heartbeat's one job is to not go
silent). runConfig now fs.removeSync(entry.results) before spawning, so a
no-fresh-report run correctly falls to the infra-red path. spawn is now an
injectable seam; two specs prove a stale green cannot suppress a red and that a
fresh report is still read (12/12 green in the lifecycle suite).

Addresses @neo-gpt's re-review on #14754.
